entry.title: Re: MIGO batch disable
entry.content:
This BAdI is intended to provide a custom dynpro in an additional tabstrip and to pass some data. Not suitable to change the field status of the standard dynpros. (Basically executed on the tabstrip part of screen)

  • You could try to look at method oref_detail_batch->pbo (include LMIGODO2) which should already contain ENHANCEMENT-POINT LMIGODO2_01 SPOTS ES_SAPLMIGO.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m Vitaly, originally from Germany, and I’ve been working in the SAP logistics field for over a decade. A while ago, I made the move to beautiful Cyprus — and I couldn’t be happier with the combination of sun, nature, and digital opportunities this island offers.

I’m especially excited to connect with others from Cyprus (or those who are planning to move here). I believe in strong communities and knowledge-sharing, so I’ve started a local SAP Community group for Cyprus. https://community.sap.com/t5/cyprus/gh-p/cyprus

Whether you're new here or have been around for a while, I’d love to connect, meet up in person, discuss SAP-related topics, and support each other in solving real-world problems.

If you're in Cyprus or feel addressed by this — feel free to reach out or join the group. Let’s make the SAP Community here even stronger!

Looking forward to hearing from you — where are you tuning in from?

Best regards from sunny Cyprus,
Vitaly

entry.title: Re: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2
entry.content:
It's always nice to know aesthetics are upgraded. I assume it's because the functionalities are working at their full potential, like the "follow user" feature.

O, wait...

entry.title: Re: End-to-End Testing with SAP Cloud ALM - Overview
entry.content:
Thank you for this very detailed blog! 😊

I was wondering if you happen to know whether it's possible to create a test case directly from a requirement. I'm aware that we can assign existing test cases to requirements, but I'm specifically looking to create a new test case in direct relation to a requirement.

The reason for this is that some scope items, such as 5HG, do not have process diagrams. As a result, it's not possible to create a manual test case for these scope items in SAP Cloud ALM. Instead of creating a self-authored process diagram—which is quite time-consuming and likely unnecessary, as we expect SAP to provide a standard best practices diagram—we’re exploring whether it’s possible to create a test case directly linked to a requirement, to which the scope item (without a process diagram) would be assigned.

Thanks in advance for any insights you can share! 

Leslie

entry.title: SAP Community Cyprus 🇨🇾 – Meet, Share, Solve!
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m Vitaly, originally from Germany, and I’ve been working in the SAP logistics field for over a decade. A while ago, I made the move to beautiful Cyprus — and I couldn’t be happier with the combination of sun, nature, and digital opportunities this island offers.

I’m especially excited to connect with others from Cyprus (or those who are planning to move here). I believe in strong communities and knowledge-sharing, so I’ve started a local SAP Community group for Cyprus. https://community.sap.com/t5/cyprus/gh-p/cyprus

Whether you're new here or have been around for a while, I’d love to connect, meet up in person, discuss SAP-related topics, and support each other in solving real-world problems.

If you're in Cyprus or feel addressed by this — feel free to reach out or join the group. Let’s make the SAP Community here even stronger!

Looking forward to hearing from you — where are you tuning in from?

Best regards from sunny Cyprus,
Vitaly

entry.title: Re: Name of iOS app is not showing correctly on iOS and MacOS
entry.content:
@timokapanen 

Dear Timo,

I have tried your instructions and they helped solve all the issues.

Thank you very much!

Best regards,

Chantal CHI

entry.title: Re: CCAP_ECN_MAINTAIN use
entry.content:
Use class  /plmi/cl_ecn_bo

 /plmi/cl_ecn_bo=>get_instance( IMPORTING eo_bo = go_ecn ).

    iv_change_no = <fs_data>-aennr.

    go_ecn->get_ecn(
     EXPORTING
       iv_change_no     = iv_change_no
       iv_refresh       = iv_refresh
       iv_lock_flag     = iv_lock_flag
       iv_include_saved = iv_include_saved
     IMPORTING
       es_ecn           = es_ecn
       et_message       = et_message
       ev_severity      = ev_severity
          ).

 

  go_ecn->lock(  EXPORTING
                    iv_change_no = iv_change_no
                   IMPORTING
                    et_message   = et_message
                    ev_severity  = ev_severity ).

 

**  do your thing e.g. change date

 go_ecn->shift_valid_from_date(
      EXPORTING
        iv_change_no      = iv_change_no
        iv_valid_from_new = iv_valid_from_new
      IMPORTING
        et_message        = et_message
        ev_severity       = ev_severity
           ).

 go_ecn->check_before_save(
      IMPORTING
        et_message  = et_message
        ev_severity = ev_severity
           ).

    go_ecn->save(
      IMPORTING
        et_message        = et_message
        ev_severity       = ev_severity
           ).

    go_ecn->unlock( iv_change_no ).


    COMMIT WORK.

  go_ecn->cleanup( '01' ).

entry.title: Re: Planning based on batch expiry date - Shelf life planning in SAP S4 HANA PPDS
entry.content:
Hello @RaghavanKathamuthu  

 

Thank you very much for your great contribution.

I have a few questions that are somewhat focused on:

1.- How will the expiry date of a lot be affected for materials in a distribution center if PPDS is not applied there, only MRP live. But if PPDS were to be implemented for the factories.

2.- Regarding the factories, how to know when to apply the data related to shelf life in the advanced planning view and/or the Plant/store 1 view.

I appreciate your valuable response regarding this.

 

Best regards

mdarsy@hotmail.com

entry.title: Re: CoA is not printing automatically after Post Goods is...
entry.content: Yes, the requirement makes sure the output is only triggered after PGI. Also you say the condition record is in place, but is is also correctly maintained (e.g. printer not missing in case of print out etc.) and you see it is found in the Determination Analysis?

entry.title: SAP Community Cyprus – Connect locally, grow globally
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm Vitaly – an SAP logistics consultant with over 13 years of experience. A while ago, I relocated to beautiful Cyprus.
Over the years, I’ve learned one thing: SAP thrives on connection, discussion, and collaboration.

That’s why I’ve started a community for SAP professionals living in Cyprus.

https://community.sap.com/t5/cyprus/gh-p/cyprus

💡 Why this group?

Because many of us are based on the island – but don’t yet know each other.
Because we can all benefit from exchanging ideas, sharing experiences, solving challenges, and supporting one another.
And because face-to-face meetups are coming back – whether over coffee in Limassol, an afterwork drink in Larnaca, or a workshop in Nicosia.

👥 Who is this for?

  • SAP consultants, developers, architects, project managers – freelance or employed, local or remote.

  • Anyone working in SAP who’s eager to share knowledge, grow their network, or simply connect.

  • Newcomers to Cyprus or long-time residents who believe in community.

🤝 What to expect?

  • Local meetups and in-person gatherings

  • Exchange on SAP topics (modules, S/4HANA, tools, experiences, etc.)

  • Peer support and open discussions

  • A friendly space to connect, grow, and inspire each other

If this speaks to you – you’re very welcome to join.

Let’s build something real and valuable – together, here on the island.

Warm regards from Cyprus,
Vitaly

entry.title: Re: How to index variant attributes to find them from search
entry.content: Hi mkysoft, thanks for your reply. I have some question: is indexing variants means modify the SolrIndexedType? (so, modify the 'composed type' from product to variants, modify the query for indexing, modify indexed properties....). Or your solution is just add the iso code of variants into an attribute of SOLR?

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content: I would like to ask where I can find the file for importing the Target System. Currently, the link provided in the article seems to be inaccessible. Is there an alternative source or updated link available?

entry.title: Note：3581811 について
entry.content:
Note：3581811 につきまして、以下、確認をお願い致します。

1.適用するに辺り、業務影響や懸念事項はありますでしょうか。
2.この脆弱性はSAP Solution Managerに対して影響をうけるのでしょうか 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Note：3581811 について
entry.content:
＞1.適用するに辺り、業務影響や懸念事項はありますでしょうか。
→業務影響がございません。3581811 - [CVE-2025-27428] に従っていただければ問題ないです。

＞2.この脆弱性はSAP Solution Managerに対して影響をうけるのでしょうか。
→ディレクトリトラバーサル脆弱性により、権限のある攻撃者がRFC対応の汎用モジュールを利用して重要な情報にアクセスする可能性があります。
攻撃が成功すると、SAP Solution Managerに接続されたあらゆる管理対象システムからファイルを読み取ることができ、機密性に重大な影響を及ぼします。整合性や可用性には影響はありません。
詳細について、KBAの以下の部分をご確認ください。
3581811 - [CVE-2025-27428] 
=>Due to directory traversal vulnerability, an authorized attacker could gain access to some critical information by using RFC enabled function module. Upon successful exploitation, they could read files from any managed system connected to SAP Solution Manager, leading to high impact on confidentiality. There is no impact on integrity or availability.

entry.title: Re: EDI - Sales Orders
entry.content:
Whether data is correct or not should not matter - your middleware company should always send the data to SAP and not keep anything behind. The idoc should be send to your SAP system and in case sales order cannot be created due to, for example, non-existing material number in the idoc, the idoc will be in error - status 51. 

You can use transaction WE02 to have visibility on these failed idocs and then take necessary actions.

entry.title: Re: Can't send documents using the Microsoft Outlook
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Due Date Calculation - NonWorking Days
entry.content:
Hi ,

There is no way to consider the holiday calendar for payment due date determination. System calculates date based on payment terms without consudering holiday calendar and weekends.

entry.content:
Found my answer through this video and statement:

Profit center - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: Syntax Error in Behavior Implementation Class for R_MATERIALDOCUMENTTP in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
I hope this message finds you well.

As part of our ongoing project to enhance the Material Document functionality, we are developing a custom event binding to incorporate specific filters and additional attributes tailored to our business requirements. Our approach thus far includes:

  1. Creation of Root Entity: Establishing the foundational entity for the Material Document.

  2. Development of Abstract Entity: Defining the abstract structure to support our custom logic.

  3. Implementation of Custom Behavior Class: Designing the behavior class to encapsulate the business logic.

  4. Service Binding: Integrating the behavior class with the service layer for seamless communication.

However, we have encountered a syntax error as (BEHAVIOR " was expected, not "EVENTS") during the activation of the behavior implementation class for the R_MATERIALDOCUMENTTP entity. Notably, the same syntax functions correctly in SAP S/4HANA 2023, but fails in the 2022 version

Upon investigation, we understand that SAP S/4HANA 2022 enforces a restricted ABAP environment, which imposes limitations on certain ABAP syntax and constructs. This environment restricts the use of dynamic programming constructs, code generation techniques, and access to non-released SAP APIs, among other constraints.

Thanks,

Prathip

entry.title: Enhancement before SAVE for Custom object changes
entry.content:
The requirement is to check for the Developer group validation before the changes are being saved in the Transport request.

Suppose Developer has made a change in the existing custom program, and he/she belong to different developer group (which is part of custom configuration), pop up should appear on screen with Question and YES / NO buttons. 

We got include LS38EF00 with form SAVE (Line No 1737) but the Object FUGR S38E cannot be extended.
Experts, please help to get the spot where we can include code which will satisfy the requirement.

Regards,
Prasanna

entry.title: Re: Inconsistencies Between Quality and Production Enviro...
entry.content:
Hi,

There might be inconsistency in TR movements to your production system. Please check the configuration comparision between Quality and Production and compare the TR list. 

 

Regards

entry.title: Understanding Joule - (Step #7) Joule for Developers
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Understanding Joule in 8 Baby Steps Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

#4 Joule Interaction Patterns

#5 Joule in SAP SuccessFactors

#6 Document Grounding in Joule

In this blog, we will learn about Joule for Developers

 

What is Joule for developers?

Joule for Developers is the umbrella term for all the design-time AI capabilities offered by SAP that help improve developer productivity and proficiency.

These AI capabilities are provided across SAP Build solutions, including ABAP.

 

A Glimpse on Joule for Developers

Let’s have a quick glimpse on the Joule for Developers

 

Joule for Developers – A Comprehensive Developer Assistance

Joule provides comprehensive developer assistance for all SAP development needs. It understands SAP development framework intricacies, anticipate developers’ needs, offer intelligent suggestions, automate repetitive, and mundane tasks like documentation and sample data generation.

It can enable developers across low-code, pro-code, and automation projects to be more productive, creative, and proficient in accelerating apps or extensions for business applications like SAP S/4HANA.

 

Key Capabilities of Joule for Developers

As shown in below image, these capabilities comprise of new and existing AI capabilities that are enabled by Joule within SAP Build Code, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and ABAP in SAP BTP and S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.

All these capabilities help developers build faster, code smarter, and automate better.

 

Here are some of the key capabilities:

 

Application creation

Generate code, UI, data models, and sample data across SAP programming models for Java, JavaScript, and ABAP, using Joule.

Below image shows Joule capabilities in SAP Build Code to help generate a data model.

 

 

Code optimization

Refactor code, create unit tests, and generate code explanations, summarizations, and more with natural language queries and intuitive actions with Joule.

Below image shows Joule capabilities in ABAP development tools to explain a CDS view.

 

Process and workflow automation 

Generate automation workflows and business rules using natural language queries.

Below image shows Joule capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation to help explain process workflows.

 

How to get FREE access to Joule for Developers?

The first thing that comes to our mind after learning about any new solution is – How can I try it for free!

Here is the good news – SAP provides few free trials for you to try Joule for Developers capabilities.

You can get started with SAP Build Code using a 30-day basic trial. This trial version can be used to test AI-based code development to generate app logic, data models, and test scripts. There is no setup required.

For more information, check SAP Build Code Free Trial.

 

What's Next?

Blog #8 Joule for Consultants [Will be published soon]

For more information on Joule for Developers, refer to:

  • Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build
  • Joule speaks ABAP!
  • Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities

entry.title: Re: I am checking the option of moving / migrating Soluti...
entry.content:
Hello Raja, 

If you speak about migration of data, only the data in SOLDOC could be migrating using the Selective Data Transfer scenario.

You have this documented in https://help.sap.com/doc/e22-sdt-sap-solution-manager-72-to-sap-cloud-alm/latest/en-US/e2e-sdt-sap-solution-manager-72-to-sap-cloud-alm_04-2025.pdf

For the other scenarios there is not any migration option delivered.

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Unable to post 0,01€ entry on VAT G/L Account
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts, 

We are currently uploading journal entries via the "Upload General Journal Entries" application (App ID: F2548), and we are encountering an issue related to the posting of €0.01 amounts to VAT G/L accounts.

These small amounts represent rounding differences generated by external interfaces and need to be cleared in SAP.

Here is the error log detail:

Tax entry not possible in this item
Message no. FF724

Diagnosis
You can not enter a tax amount in this item since, due to the posting amount entered and the tax code, a total tax amount of zero has been calculated.

This could either be due to the fact that the tax code has been defined with a value of 0%, or because the posting amount entered (that is, the tax base) is too small.

Example:

With a tax rate of 15%, a tax amount greater than zero will only be achieved with a base amount greater than 0.04. With values of 0.03 and less, it is therefore not possible to enter a tax amount.

Procedure
Only enter tax amounts in conjunction with tax codes for which a percentage rate not equal to zero has been defined. Make sure that in documents with several G/L account items you enter the total tax amount in an item with a sufficiently high posting amount.

Can you please advise on a workaround for this issue ? 

Thank you for your help. 

Best regards,

entry.title: Annotation @AbapCatalog.extensibility.allowNewDatasources: false is not working in ABAP CDS
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am trying to create a CDS view extension on CDS View Entity /DMO/E_Agency. It looks like the following - 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#PROJECTION_LIST]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Agency - Extension Include View'

@AbapCatalog.extensibility: {
  extensible: true,
  elementSuffix: 'ZAG',
  allowNewDatasources: false,
  dataSources: ['Agency'],
  quota: {
    maximumFields: 500,
    maximumBytes: 50000
  }
}
define view entity /DMO/E_Agency
  as select from /dmo/agency as Agency
{
  key agency_id as AgencyId
}

Here allowNewDatasources: false means I can't select data from other data source but I have written the following view extension wherein I am associating data from another custom table zzextenagent_swa.

extend view entity /DMO/E_Agency 
       with association to zzextenagent_swa as _zzswas 
    on $projection.AgencyId = _zzswas.agencyid       
{     
     _zzswas      
}

And now I am able to include my custom table field to the main CDS view like below. 

extend view entity /DMO/R_AgencyTP with {
    _Extension._zzswas.zagswascity as ZZSWASCITYZAG
}

Here /DMO/R_AgencyTP looks like below where /DMO/E_Agency works as an extension include view.

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Agency'
@Search.searchable: true

@AbapCatalog.extensibility: {
  extensible: true,
  elementSuffix: 'ZAG',
  allowNewDatasources: false,
  dataSources: ['_Extension'],
  quota: {
    maximumFields: 500,
    maximumBytes: 50000
  },
  allowNewCompositions: true
}

define root view entity /DMO/R_AgencyTP
  as select from /DMO/I_Agency as Agency
  association [0..1] to I_Country     as _Country   on $projection.CountryCode = _Country.Country
  association [1]    to /DMO/E_Agency as _Extension on $projection.AgencyID = _Extension.AgencyId
{
....

Essentially I am able to add my custom table fields to this standard CDS view despite allowNewDatasources: false.

So, my question is then is this annotation not working correctly or I am misunderstanding something here. Tagging experts @Andre_Fischer @OlgaDolinskaja @CarineTchoutouo @ThomasSchneider 

Thanks,
Mainak

entry.title: How Signavio Process Analysis and Mining helps to deal with shortage of SAP ERP consultants
entry.content:
Many people argue about the shortage of S/4 consultants and limited knowledge and how to run and optimize SAP processes in an ever changing IT world.

The answer is an app which is simply accumulating and structuring the insights and knowledge of thousands of consulting hours, out-of-the-box, for every SAP solution.

Signavio Process Analysis and Mining provides even two apps for this problem which are leaders in Gartner's magic quadrant since 3 years in a row:

  • Signavio Process Insights 
  • Signavio Process Intelligence

 

 

 

Below you find a demo how to leverage both solutions including the out-of-the-box analysis content from SAP and partners.

entry.title: Embracing the Future: Navigating the Transition with SAP Enable Now & WalkMe
entry.content:
At SAP, we are constantly imagining and crafting the future to ensure that our customers stay at the forefront of innovation and efficiency. With this vision, we are excited to share some important updates regarding SAP Enable Now—a tool that has empowered many diverse organizations to manage their digital learning landscapes.

This blog post consists of three parts:

  1. SAP Enable Now Lifecycle Updates
  2. Caring for Our Customers: Transitioning Smoothly
  3. Exploring Target Use Cases for In-App Digital Adoption and Digital Learning

As we move towards new horizons, our planned transition marks an earnest step in bringing a seamless and integrated digital learning experience to life. In alignment with our commitment to transparency and support, we want to assure our important and integral customers that existing contracts within the maintenance timeframe will be honored. Furthermore, for those with contracts extending beyond our defined end of maintenance dates, rest assured that you will be contacted separately to discuss personalized transition approaches.

Our future solution has already been pre-announced in the blog post: Reimagining digital learning as an integrated part of a Digital Adoption Platform with SAP & WalkMe. We encourage you to explore this vision that promises to integrate digital learning smoothly into everyday workflows. The new WalkMe solution, expected to be generally available in Q4 of 2025, will be SAP’s go-forward solution for digital learning - purpose-built to go beyond traditional learning systems by embedding personalized, AI-powered training experiences directly into the flow of work.

If you’re interested to learn more about WalkMe and the new vision for digital learning, register for WalkMe Realize on June 17th! At WalkMe Realize on June 17th, the brightest minds in digital adoption and transformation come together for one powerful day of ideas, insights, and conversations that shape the future of work. If you’re driving change in your organization, this is where you need to be.

The new WalkMe solution will enable organizations to develop, oversee, and provide contextual training precisely when it's needed—across any device, in diverse formats, and integrated into any workflow. This will go beyond simply adapting eLearning for business applications; it will offer tailored learning that directly caters to users' needs, driven by smart AI authoring tools, personalized recommendations, and hands-on simulation practice. It will bridge the gap between acquiring knowledge and applying it, all seamlessly incorporated into daily work processes through the leading digital adoption platform.

To get started, let’s focus on SAP Enable Now and important lifecycle dates coming up.

1. SAP Enable Now Lifecycle Updates

Here's a closer look at the timeline as we gear towards new beginnings:

  • SAP Enable Now, cloud edition
    • As of April 22nd, 2025, SAP is no longer initiating new contracts.
    • Starting November 30th, 2026, existing contracts will not be eligible for renewal.
    • End of maintenance scheduled for November 30th 2030. Extended maintenance will not be offered.
  • SAP Enable Now, enablement content creation edition (interim)
    • Starting January 2026, SAP is no longer initiating new contracts.
    • Starting November 30th, 2026, existing contracts will not be eligible for renewal.
    • End of maintenance scheduled for November 30th, 2030. Extended maintenance will not be offered.
  • SAP Enable Now, consumption edition & authoring edition (on-premise) & PCE / PTO editions 
    • As of April 22nd, 2025, SAP is no longer initiating new contracts.
    • End of mainstream maintenance set for November 30th, 2030.
    • Existing on-premise customers will transition into customer specific maintenance automatically.
    • PCE / PTO contracts will not be renewed.

The maintenance related dates are available in the Product Availability Matrix (PAM) for easy reference. See SAP Maintenance Phases for more details on the maintenance phases.

2. Caring for Our Customers: Transitioning Smoothly

We understand that transitions can pose questions and concerns. That's why we've designed commercial and technical transition options for existing SAP Enable Now customers with care and precision. These options will be thoroughly evaluated by a Customer Sounding Board in the coming months and their feedback will shape the final version.

While both commercial and technical transition plans will follow a standard path, if necessary, transition paths can be tailored on a case-by-case basis. This ensures that we meet the unique needs and aspirations of each customer. Each customer can transition on their own time within the SAP Enable Now contract duration.

Here’s what we can tell you already about the transition of digital learning content:

  • Playback Integration: It is planned to provide a feature within the new WalkMe offering to seamlessly link all existing digital learning content from SAP Enable Now, allowing easy access to and usage of WalkMe’s playback capabilities while preserving the current content library.
  • Content Migration and Editing: WalkMe is planning to transfer content from the existing SAP Enable Now tenant to a new server, making legacy content accessible in WalkMe’s content management module. Editing legacy content is planned to be possible.
  • Full Transition and Testing: Customers will be asked to test the migrated content within the new WalkMe offering to ensure functionality. User profiles, authorizations, and Single-Sign-On are to be set up, ensuring smooth access and contribution to digital learning resources while concluding usage of SAP Enable Now.

There are currently no plans to automatically transition the in-app content built with SAP Enable Now but other options are being evaluated.

SAP is planning to offer a "Test & Go Live" license type for customers to cover the transition period. See the illustration below to better understand how the transition will work contractually. More information on this license type will be available soon.

 More detailed information on the transition options will be published in due time.

3. Exploring Target Use Cases for In-App Digital Adoption and Digital Learning

In the digital transformation era, organizations are leveraging advanced tools to enhance user engagement and learning experiences. Two critical use cases here are digital adoption through in-app solutions and digital learning. Recognizing these use cases and their nuances allows businesses to select the appropriate technology, viewing them as complementary solutions that prioritize people at the heart of successful digital transformation, and ultimately driving growth and innovation. Let's dive into each use case, explore practical examples, and illustrate how WalkMe offers the right solution for each use case.

a) In-App Digital Adoption

The first use case revolves around enhancing user experience through in-app digital adoption platforms (DAPs). These tools integrate directly with existing software applications, in-app guidance, automation and interactive walkthroughs in the flow of work. The objective is to simplify processes, boost productivity, and reduce onboarding time.

Example Use Cases:

  • Enhancing ERP Systems: Companies implementing complex ERP solutions often face challenges in helping people navigate the new technology, driving adoption and ensuring key workflows are run as intended. With WalkMe Premium / DAP, firms can offer real-time guidance and walkthroughs that simplify the user journey within applications and optimize process execution, reducing errors and support ticket volumes.
  • CRM Utilization: Sales teams using Customer Relationship Management systems can leverage in-app guidance to streamline deal management, forecasting and CPQ. By providing guidance in the moment of need with automations of key repetitive tasks, WalkMe aids in maximizing CRM utilization, driving faster lead-to-deal conversions.

With in-app guidance, companies have seen 60% faster adoption of new applications and features, 45% faster application migrations and 41% fewer business process errors (source: IDC White Paper, The Business Value of WalkMe).

b) Enablement Content Creation & Digital Learning

The second use case focuses on creating rich digital learning content aimed at skill development and mastery using comprehensive training modules. This use case involves developing e-learning content, simulations, and empowering tutorials creating learning experiences designed to upskill the workforce.

Example Use Cases:

  • Corporate Training Programs: Organizations crafting structured training programs can utilize enablement content creation platforms to develop courses, quizzes, and simulations tailored to specific roles or departments. This not only standardizes critical knowledge across teams but also fosters future leadership and skill development.
  • Compliance and Regulation Training: Firms in highly regulated industries benefit from detailed digital learning modules that ensure compliance training is thorough and engaging. Through interactive scenarios and comprehensive assessments, employees can navigate complex regulatory landscapes with confidence and agility.

While SAP Enable Now has been instrumental in enabling content creation, the upcoming WalkMe offering for digital learning promises to evolve this capability further, allowing companies to design innovative learning experiences that are immersive, scalable, and intuitive.

c) Conclusion:

In-app digital adoption focuses on real-time user guidance within applications - ideal for immediate, task-oriented support. Digital learning centers around structured knowledge dissemination and skill development over time, which is crucial for comprehensive employee growth and regulatory adherence.

Both use cases present unique advantages tailored to specific organizational needs. By leveraging tools like WalkMe Premium / DAP and upcoming digital learning solutions, enterprises can effectively address process inefficiencies and elevate learning experiences, ensuring that both users and systems operate at their highest potential.

For a comprehensive digital strategy, the best possible option is for customers to cover both use cases with WalkMe in the future. This approach promotes a seamless and interactive digital enablement environment that supports real-time digital adoption while facilitating robust digital learning. By doing so, organizations can ensure that employees not only receive immediate, practical support within applications but also engage in transformational learning experiences, leading to enhanced productivity, skill mastery, and organizational success.

Stay Informed and Connected

Follow me, this blog, and the SAP Enable Now tag on the SAP Community to stay informed about updates.

We know you may have questions, and our team is ready to assist. Please feel free to reach out via the Q&A section below or connect with your SAP representative directly.

Looking Ahead Together

This initiative isn't just about transitioning; it's about evolving. We're dedicated to walking this exciting journey with you, as we craft a future where digital learning isn't a separate task but a naturally integrated part of everyday work. 

As we turn the page to this new chapter, we remain inspired by the possibilities and motivated by the partnership we've shared. Together, let's embrace the future with open arms and shape the digital learning landscape for generations to come.

Thank you for being a member of our community - a future filled with promise awaits us!

entry.title: SAP SOLMAN CHARM Digital Signature Fucntionality
entry.content:

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP SOLMAN CHARM Digital Signature Fucntionality
entry.content:
As documented in KBA 1902352 - Digital Signature with Change Request Management (ChaRM) - Solution Manager, there is not an out-of-the-box solution for digital signature within ChaRM. Currently, the only available solutions for this are from consulting.

Digital Signature functionality can be integrated with Focused Build.

entry.title: Does SAP recommend to have same PSE for both SAP SSL Server Standard and SAP SSL Client ?
entry.content: Does SAP recommend to have same PSE for both SAP SSL Server Standard and SAP SSL Client Standard in STRUST ? Can we copy the SSL Server PSE to SSL Client Standard ? If so, is this recommended by SAP ?

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
This comment is a placeholder for SAP MBC Multi Bank Connectivity...

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity | SAP Help Portal

 

 

 

ERP to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

 

When your ERP is connected to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity, you can review the status of files transferred to banks or FSIs directly in your ERP system. You can even connect several ERP systems if this is your use case. This should be aligned with your business and/or banks and FSIs as part of the onboarding process with your dedicated onboarding team.

You set up the connection between ERP and SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity via the connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. It's a module designed to interact with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity to standardize the integration. The connector is independent of the technical connectivity from ERP to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. As part of the onboarding to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity, you need to consider different configurations for the connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. The configurations depend on the ERP system you use. For more information, please see the relevant topics in the Onboarding section.

 

SFTP (H2H)

With this connection, you can send messages from the corporate system to the corporate tenant and then to a bank backend system.

For integrating your tenant directly to your bank, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity offers connectivity via Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) as it provides simple and effective scalability options.

In this scenario, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity pushes data to a bank's hosted server, and SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity pulls response data from the bank's server.

In most cases, the bank hosts SFTP servers for message exchange. If this isn't possible, the customer or their service partners might need to host the server. SAP doesn't host SFTP servers.

 

Onboarding Process | SAP Help Portal

 

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity - OnBoarding Resource Center

Using SAP for Me to Provision Systems | SAP Help Portal

Onboarding Process | SAP Help Portal

Connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity | SAP Help Portal

Roles and Responsibilities in Onboarding | SAP Help Portal

 

AL11 

In MBC you will need to store Bank Files on the S/4HANA File System and you will need ECS to make a File System available and permissioned and accessible with AL11..

3435333 - AL11 Usage, Restrictions, and Support for HEC/ECS Managed Systems - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: I am checking the option of moving / migrating Soluti...
entry.content:
Hi!

I would suggest that you run a SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM to see what you are using in your SolMan system and which scenarios are delivered in the SAP Cloud ALM, see SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM guidance here:
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/sap-readiness-check-for-sap-cloud-alm.html

See also SAP Activate for Transition to SAP Cloud ALM
https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapoverviewpage/dbe443042b8f43d085c9e5e221bef757

Then check the content in 

- Transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm.html

- Transition to SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-calm-implementation.html

- Transition to SAP Cloud ALM: Guidance for typical customer situations
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-calm-example-scenarios.html

- Selective data transfer from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-calm-implementation.html?anchorId=section_284292647_co

- Guidance for Transitioning from SAP Solution Manager Change Request Management to SAP Cloud ALM Change
& Deployment Management
https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/alm/sap-cloud-alm/Best_Practice_Guide_Transition_ChaRM_to_SAP_Cloud_ALM_v1_08042025.pdf

Hope this helps,

Dolores

entry.title: How to pass BASE64 image data to smartform graphic or any other option to get the image in invoice
entry.content:
I have an image of a QR code which contain data 'A'. I am working on a Smart form, here in the invoice I need to get this data 'A' when the user is scanning the QR code.

The above-mentioned Image is in a BASE64 data. How can I make it appear in invoice through Smart form.

I have tried decoding the data and converting it to Binary, but I was not able to covert that binary to BITMAP data as I got exception saying No BITMAP file. However, even if I get that bitmap data also I couldn't find a way to bind it in graphic node, as there is no option to select graphic type in Smart form.

FM which I have used 

SCMS_BASE64_DECODE_STR

SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY

SAPSCRIPT_CONVERT_BITMAP

entry.title: Re: Issue with L_TO_CREATE_MULTIPLE , Global Data is not coming informed (T333, T340,....)
entry.content:
Hello @luisvazquez 

The T333_LESEN form routine simply reads WM movement definition from T333 and T333T tables:

FORM T333_LESEN USING VALUE(P_LGNUM) VALUE(P_BWLVS).

  SELECT SINGLE * FROM T333
  WHERE LGNUM = P_LGNUM    AND
        BWLVS = P_BWLVS.

  IF SY-SUBRC NE 0.
    MESSAGE E001 WITH P_BWLVS RAISING BWLVS_WRONG.
  ENDIF.

  PERFORM T333T_LESEN USING P_LGNUM P_BWLVS.

ENDFORM.

As you see, the logic is extremely simple. If that doesn't work for you, it means that the WM movement type is not customized for the warehouse number. Check the content of T333.

What is the specific error that you get?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Analytics Cloud & Business ByDesign Business Content (AP-RC-ANA-SAC) Errors in queries & import jobs
entry.content:
Hi, everyone.

I am currently trying to implement the SAP Business ByDesign business content (AP-RC-ANA-SAC) available in SAP Analytics Cloud. I am using the connector that comes with this business content (SAP_BYD_CONNECTOR), but I am having a problem with some model import jobs. Namely, the premade import jobs for models SAP__BYD_FI_ORDERPROFIT and SAP__BYD_FI_EARNEDPROFIT do not work. They provide the following error: The freehand query is not an entity set or function import" when creating a new Freehand query in SAC

 

I tried remaking the import jobs myself, by copying a query from a model. In the image below, after pressing next with this or a similar configuration, I am met with an infinite loading screen.

 

Thus, I tried making a new query. The problem is that the the tables for the two aforementioned models, RPFINPRFU30_Q0004QueryResults and RPFINPRFU30_Q0006QueryResults, are not visible in the list of tables, no matter what I do.

 

 

Do you have any advice on what I need to do in order to get access to these tables? My knowledge of ByDesign is quite limited, but the connector in SAC seems to be implemented correctly. I also should have access to Corporate Performance Analytics, since this connector does not return an authentication error.


...sapbydesign.com/sap/byd/odata/cpm_corporateperformance_analytics.svc

Can anyone guide How to fix it?

entry.title: Re: Fiori Launchpad Plugin (using setHeaderTitle) not working
entry.content: Hi Ankit, were you able to identify the root cause of the issue?? for this error? --> Filed to load JavaScript resource: sap/ushell/plugins/rta/Component-preload.js - sap.ui.ModuleSystem

entry.title: Re: Withholding Tax calculation issue while posting REFX...
entry.content:
Hi Tivahar,

 

Firstly you will need to create 2 withholding tax items in the Term of condition. In the posting item of the term, you will have to choose multiple suppliers, change the layout to bring in withholding tax into the posting terms. Here you will have to individual assign the WHT terms you created to each individual landlord. The layout will not default to ask you the WHT terms when splitting the condition with 2 suppliers.

Regards,

Lakshmi

entry.title: Re: Enhancement before SAVE for Custom object changes
entry.content: Hi Raymond,

entry.title: Re: Enhancement before SAVE for Custom object changes
entry.content: Hi Raymond, I checked the mentioned BAdI, but it is triggering ata the time of TR released.

entry.title: Poland VAT exchange rate in Account Payables process with Purchase order (SAP ECC)
entry.content:
Hi, in Poland if domestic invoice is received in foreign currency, invoice needs to have VAT value in PLN currency (official Polish currency).  Invoice date is not necessarily a match with this VAT f/x rate used in the invoice as it can be based on delivery date.  

Is there a way to solve this in SAP ECC that VAT can have different exchange rate date than invoice date or posting date? 

There is Note for S/4 Hana public cloud version that I think is suitable but I did not find any for ECC.

entry.title: Re: Enhancement before SAVE for Custom object changes
entry.content: Hi Raymond, Thank You for reply. I checked the mentioned BAdI, but it is triggering at the time of TR release. We need before saving the changes to TR.

My client mentioned that  they cannot access the Onboarding Survey which should be available from a Custom Task called "Candidate Experience at Daikin". Here you may see the MDF Object name (Recruiting survey link (cust_surveyRecruiting).

We cannot reach that even trying to access the link to Survey from our internal sharepoint (it's throwing an error this form does not exist. Please make sure the link is correct. 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Sourcing and Procuremant
entry.content:
Hi,

It is not available.

For managing discounts or differences, SAP S/4HANA might include features like Discount Management via the SAP Business Network, which offers functionality Sourcing and Procurement - Discount Management (42K) to transfer payment proposals and manage cash discount terms. Such features help in providing insights into payment and discount dynamics but may not directly report differences between quotation and order amounts for specific items.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Become an Early Adopter for Joule in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
If I understood it correctly, in a nutshell, is that this early access program is for customers only.

Partners, who are often asked by customers and expect us to inform them about new technology, must wait, or convince a customer of ours to sign up.

entry.title: Re: how to make an Optional inspection characteristic Required after a certain number of inspection
entry.content: @memem : Yes thanks. That is the approach which finally worked for me. I had been struggling with it because I had been maintaining the char as optional but once I mentioned it as required and then applied DMR at char level, it worked perfectly.

entry.title: Re: BOP - S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, materials with project stock might not behave the same way as regular stock during the Backorder Processing (BOP) job due to the inherent restrictions and configurations. This behavior you observed is indeed related to how SAP handles project stocks in comparison to regular stocks.

How Project Stock Works

Project stock is treated distinctly from plant stock, and its availability is tightly linked to enterprise projects. The primary goal of project stock is to ensure that materials are consumed only by the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2108 and assigned orders to which the project stock belongs, enhancing planning efficiency and project procurement effectiveness source 1.

Availability Check Configuration

The difference in behavior between project stock and regular stock during BOP can be influenced by availability check configurations, scope of check, and other setting variants used in your system.

Things to Consider

  • Scope of Check: Ensure that the scope of check is properly configured to include project stock. Sometimes project stock might require specific settings to be included in the availability check.
  • Configuration Settings: Check if there are settings in the Availability Check or Backorder Processing that might apply only to regular stock and not project stock.

Possible Adjustments

You may consider reviewing and adjusting the configuration settings such as:

  • Order Promising Strategies: Supply Chain - Backorder Processing (BOP) Evaluate if the settings for project stock in ‘Order Promising’ and availability checks are correctly set source 12.
  • Backorder Management: Ensure that high-running materials settings are well configured Supply Chain - High-Running Materials in Backorder Processing for project stock and adjust the BOP configurations appropriately source 13.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Enhancing IDoc Filtering in SAP BD64
entry.content: Informative! Just a question - is it possible for dynamic idoc filtering? For example if you put filter 'W101' for plant it will only send the idoc with that plant. But how about all plants that start with W, like 'W*', would that be possible?

entry.title: Re: Name of iOS app is not showing correctly on iOS and MacOS
entry.content: Excellent to hear, thanks for confirming @ChantalChi

entry.title: Re: Can't instantiate a fragment after Destory it
entry.content:
I suggest to avoid destroying it to avoid that error.

openDocumentaleDialog: function () {
    if (!this.oDocumentaleDialog) {
        Fragment.load({
            name: "webapp.fragment.DocumentaleDialog",
            id: this.createId("dialogDocumentale"),
            controller: this
        }).then((oFragment) => {
            this.oDocumentaleDialog = oFragment;
            this.getView().addDependent(this.oDocumentaleDialog);
            this.oDocumentaleDialog.open();
        });
    } else {
        this.oDocumentaleDialog.open();
    }
}

 Your assumption "To ensure good performance, I simply destroy it" — is partially correct as recreating the fragment from scratch does have a cost. And to be fair, a popup is usually something very minor when it comes to performance and memory overhead.

entry.title: Fetching the Count from OData service response
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am building an application using SAP Build Apps, integrating it with SAP SuccessFactors Platform with OData services.

On a view, I turned on the Include total count feature from the Paging option, found on the Get Record Collection flow logic.

With this, I get the data from SAP SuccessFactor, in below format:
{
"d": {
"results": [ ... ],
"__count": "381"
}
}

Can anyone please help me understand, how can I use/bind the value coming in the field - __count , so that I can display the count of the records?

Regards,

Chandan

entry.title: Re: Maintenance notifications app short dump
entry.content:
So create an incident for reach support.

  • 2733141 - Attribution of Runtime Errors from /BOBF/ Programs

entry.title: Re: Consuming data from SAP Datasphere into Power BI via ODBC connector
entry.content:
Hi  @vignesh3027 ,
Can you help me with importing Variables(Standard variable to be precise) created in Datasphere ? 

I tried the following steps :

 https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/passing-values-in-input-parameters-variables-of-datasphere-model-from-power/ba-p/13988127 .


But, I keep running into the following error = > DataSource.Error: ODBC: ERROR [S1000] [SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB DLL][HDBODBC] General error;303 invalid DATE, TIME or TIMESTAMP value: SQL Error 

Details:
    DataSourceKind=Odbc
    DataSourcePath=dsn

More on this, I have tried disabling and enabling global/current page Time intelligence for auto detection of Data field.

entry.title: Re: Enhancement before SAVE for Custom object changes
entry.content: Then ou could look for some Authorization check (on object S_DEVELOP)

entry.title: Re: Support of SAP Build Work Zone, advanced and much more: SAP Mobile Start V2.2 & 2.3 - Releas
entry.content: Great release update, @ThiloBerndt! 🚀  SAP Mobile Start v2.3 now supports Android 16, so our users can immediately enjoy an optimized experience as soon as Android 16 becomes available. Stay tuned for more!

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers
entry.content:
Hi @Sanika_Kate ,

It looks like you did not complete the verification process. Can you revisit your verification session and complete it?

entry.title: Re: How students can get certified free and FAST in SAP (Less than 7 hours!)
entry.content:
Sure @arymak87 , no problem at all!
If you’d like, feel free to connect with me via LinkedIn  happy to stay in touch and support however I can.

Best regards,
María

entry.title: Re: Template Allocation and Splitting Structure
entry.content: Hi, I am not familiar with the formula you are using. I would suggest looking at the following function: K_ABC_GET_FILL_ACDOCA_VALS. There should be some sap notes that suggests some uses. In regards to the splitting, I can assure you that there should be no difference between using a template or other posted direct activity type. May I suggest to verify if all accounts are distributed equally or only one or a few? Usually it splits the accounts that are not mapped in the splitting schema (or cost element group if used)

entry.title: Re: Modeling an advanced Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @abarros123 if you mean selecting nodes/leafs from a hierarchy in a variable: not at this point. What is on the roadmap in this direction (but not the same) is hierarchy node selection for restricted measures

entry.title: Re: Poland VAT exchange rate in Account Payables process...
entry.content:
Is the information in KBA 1907011 - Poland Exchange Rate Requirements complementary information compliant?

  • 1330330 - Poland Exchange Rate Requirements

entry.title: Re: New Webinar Series: Architecting Your SAP Business Data Cloud Journey – From Data to Intelligenc
entry.content: Hi @EsaNieminen  - the registered participants will get access to the recordings and the slides via email. We will be posting synopsis of the session as blog posts in the coming days. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: CDS view performance Annotation for CDS view performance
entry.content:
The blog post brings up some interesting annotations, which I totally ignored in the last years.

There is no magic turbo switch!
please refer to the comment of topic expert @AndreaUS

If you experience varying performance I would see machine resources as the first reason.
If you experience totally bad performance I would use a trace and planviz  to find the root cause. Spoiler alert: 99 % chance your custom view was built in the wrong way. PEBKAC 😅 

kind regards,

Christian

entry.title: Re: EDI - Sales Orders
entry.content: To add to this answer you can configure it so failed IDocs trigger workflow for appropriate business users too in order to correct data issues, which is what we do here.

entry.title: ABAP CDS: Intent based navigation isn't working properly in draft-enabled Apps
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am currently facing an issue with intent-based navigation in ABAP CDS and Fiori Elements.

I’m using the navigation in a composition of the header table. So, when I open a detail entry of the header table and navigate further using intent-based navigation, everything works perfectly — as long as I’m not in edit mode. As soon as I switch to edit mode, the property "IsActiveEntity=false" is also passed along in the intent-based navigation. This can be correct if a draft entry exists for the navigation target. However, if there is no draft entry in the navigation target, the navigation leads to a dead end:

Here's the code of the parent-entity (Please note that the underlying service is an OData V4 service):

@EndUserText.label: 'Parent entity foo'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define root view entity ZP_PARENT_FOO
    provider contract transactional_query
    as projection on ZI_PARENT_FOO

{   
    
  key uuid                                          as UUID,
      somefield                                     as Some_Field,
      validfrom                                     as Valid_From,
      validto                                       as valid_To,

      _ChildFoo : redirected to composition child ZP_CHILD_FOO
      
}

Here's the code of the child-entity:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Child entity Foo'

define view entity ZP_CHILD_FOO
    as projection on ZI_CHILD_FOO
{

    @UI.hidden: true
    key uuid                as UUID,
    
    @UI: {
          lineItem:       [ { position: 10,
                              type: #WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION, 
                              semanticObject: 'SomeOtherApp',
                              semanticObjectAction: 'manage',
                              semanticObjectBinding: [{  localElement: 'FooUUID', element: 'UUID'} ] ,
                              requiresContext: false 
                          } ]
        }
    key Foo_uuid       as FooUUID,
    
    some_field         as SomeField,
        
    @UI.hidden: true
    last_changed_at         as LastChangedAt,
    
    _ParentFoo : redirected to parent ZP_PARENT_FOO
    
}

Is there a specific annotation that can be used to override the “IsActiveEntity” property?

Many thanks in advance and best regards
Leif

@Andre_Fischer 

SAP BTP ABAP environment ABAP Development

entry.title: Re: SF - Story Reporting - Time Tracking - Display of weekdays
entry.content:
Hi Pedro

Unforunately I haven't found a solution nor workaround. I am still looking for a way to "show" the weekdays. 

Kind regards, 

Alexandra

entry.title: Dismantle Machine
entry.content:
Q. what is the standard SAP Process of Dismantle Machine 

How to Dismantle Machine With Serial Number Profile as per Confirmation Component,

entry.title: Re: PO change in SAP
entry.content: SPED is not PO
